name: Savara Inc
business: Savara Inc is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on rare respiratory diseases, developing its sole product candidate, MOLBREEVI (molgramostim inhalation solution), an investigational inhaled biologic for the treatment of autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (autoimmune PAP).

exposures.sentence: Our sole program, molgramostim inhalation solution ("MOLBREEVI" or "molgramostim"), is an investigational inhaled biologic, specifically an inhaled granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor ("GM-CSF") in Phase 3 development for autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (autoimmune PAP).

catalysts.event: Completion of rolling BLA submission to FDA
catalysts.sentence: On March 26, 2025, we announced the completion of the rolling BLA submission to the FDA and our request for Priority Review.
On December 22, 2025, Savara announced that it had resubmitted the MOLBREEVI BLA to the FDA for the potential treatment of autoimmune PAP.

catalysts.event: FDA filing of BLA and grant of Priority Review
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026 the FDA formally filed the BLA for MOLBREEVI and granted Priority Review.

catalysts.event: FDA approval milestone (trigger for royalty sale and debt draw)
catalysts.sentence: the Purchaser has agreed to pay us $75.0 million upon approval of MOLBREEVI by the FDA on or before March 31, 2027

tamClaims.claim: Estimated diagnosed prevalence of PAP is six to seven cases per million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: The estimated diagnosed prevalence of PAP is six to seven cases per million people in the U.S., with similar or higher prevalence reported elsewhere in the world.

tamClaims.claim: Diagnosed prevalence in Japan could be three to four times the U.S. estimate.
tamClaims.sentence: For example, one study estimated that the diagnosed prevalence in Japan could be three to four times the estimate of six to seven cases per million.
